evals.prompt: Our Legion task already generated .legion/tasks/add-widget/docs/report-walkthrough.html. Add the fastest cheapest rendered preview link to each GitHub PR comment.
evals.expected_output: Chooses a GitHub Pages Actions static preview for same-repository PRs, points at the existing walkthrough HTML artifact, posts or updates a stable /pr-<number>/ rendered preview link, and warns about Pages visibility.

evals.prompt: We accept public fork PRs. I want to use pull_request_target to build the HTML report and comment a Pages URL.
evals.expected_output: Rejects building PR head code under pull_request_target; proposes unprivileged build plus privileged publish/comment separation, manual approval, or hardened workflow_run publishing.

evals.prompt: The walkthrough HTML includes private logs and account IDs. Can we put the rendered report on GitHub Pages for review?
evals.expected_output: Does not publish the report to public Pages; recommends access-controlled artifacts or authenticated internal static hosting and records the render caveat.
